title: Fahrenheit to Celsius Converter
shortDescription: Convert Fahrenheit to Celsius with a precise formula, clear unit handling, and instant results.
metaDescription: Convert Fahrenheit values to Celsius instantly with a clear formula and easy-to-read results.
intro: Enter a Fahrenheit value and get the Celsius equivalent immediately.
overview:
Use this tool to convert temperature values from Fahrenheit to Celsius with the standard formula: subtract 32, then multiply by 5/9. It is useful when you need to interpret weather readings, recipe temperatures, appliance settings, or temperature logs without doing the math by hand.

The result is shown in Celsius so you can compare temperatures in metric units right away. If you enter decimal Fahrenheit values, the converter handles them as numbers and returns a matching decimal Celsius result.
howItWorks: Enter a Fahrenheit temperature value into the input field.
The tool applies the Fahrenheit-to-Celsius formula automatically.
Read the Celsius result and reuse it for notes, comparisons, or records.

faq.question: What formula does this converter use?
faq.answer: It uses the standard formula: Celsius = (Fahrenheit - 32) × 5/9.

faq.question: Can I enter decimal temperatures?
faq.answer: Yes. Values like 72.5°F are accepted and converted to a decimal Celsius result.

faq.question: Why does the Celsius result sometimes include decimals?
faq.answer: Many Fahrenheit values do not map to whole Celsius numbers, so a decimal result is mathematically correct.

faq.question: What mistake should I avoid when reading the result?
faq.answer: Do not confuse the sign or units. A Fahrenheit input always produces a Celsius output, so the displayed number is not still in °F.
